Package org.eventb.core.tests.pom

Class PSUpdateTests

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.tests.BuilderTest
org.eventb.core.tests.pom.PSUpdateTests
public class PSUpdateTests extends BuilderTest
Tests ensuring that PS files are properly updated when the AutoPOM tool is run.

  • Field Summary

    Fields inherited from class org.eventb.core.tests.BuilderTest

    PLUGIN_ID

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    PSUpdateTests()
     

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    defaultFactoryChangedToSpecialized()
    Ensure that a non-empty proof becomes invalid when updating a proof obligation without really changing it, if PO factory changes.
    void
    emptyProofHasNofactory()
    Ensures that an empty proof has no formula factory.
    void
    specializedFactoryChangedToDefault()
    Ensure that a non-empty proof becomes invalid when updating a proof obligation without really changing it, if PO factory changes and that it gets recovered when resetting the PO factory.
    void
    specializedFactoryChangedToDefaultOnException()
    Ensure that a non-empty proof becomes invalid when updating a proof obligation without really changing it, if PO factory can't be loaded and that it gets recovered when resetting the PO factory.
    void
    specializedFactoryNotChanged()
    Ensure that a proof is kept valid when updating its proof obligation without really changing it nor its factory, even when using a specialized factory.
    void
    testContextDependentProof()
    Verify that context dependent proof statuses are updated when the builder runs, even if the PO stamp did not change.
    final void
    testEmpty()
    Ensures that an empty PO file is properly processed.
    final void
    testEmptyAddOne()
    Ensures that adding one PO to an empty PO file is properly processed.
    final void
    testEmptyAddTwo()
    Ensures that adding two POs to an empty PO file is properly processed.
    final void
    testErroneousProof()
    Ensures that a damaged proof does not prevent POM from succeeding.
    final void
    testErroneousProofDependency()
    Ensures that a damaged proof dependency does not prevent POM from succeeding.
    final void
    testErroneousProofInput()
    Ensures that a proof with a damaged input does not prevent POM from succeeding.
    final void
    testOne()
    Ensures that a PO file containing one PO is properly processed.
    final void
    testOneAddAfter()
    Ensures that adding one PO after the only PO of a PO file is properly processed.
    final void
    testOneAddBefore()
    Ensures that adding one PO before the only PO of a PO file is properly processed.
    final void
    testOneDelete()
    Ensures that deleting the only PO of a PO file is properly processed.
    final void
    testSixPermute_1()
    Ensures that inverse permuting the only six POs of a PO file is properly processed.
    final void
    testSixPermute_2()
    Ensures that permuting the only six POs of a PO file is properly processed (case with a random permutation with random deletions).
    final void
    testSixPermute_3()
    Ensures that permuting the only six POs of a PO file is properly processed (case with a random permutation with random deletions).
    final void
    testSixPermute_4()
    Ensures that permuting the only six POs of a PO file is properly processed (case with a random permutation with random deletions).
    final void
    testThreeDeleteAll()
    Ensures that deleting all POs of a PO file containing three POs is properly processed.
    final void
    testThreeDeleteFirst()
    Ensures that deleting the first PO of a PO file containing three POs is properly processed.
    final void
    testThreeDeleteLast()
    Ensures that deleting the last PO of a PO file containing three POs is properly processed.
    final void
    testThreeDeleteSecond()
    Ensures that deleting the second PO of a PO file containing three POs is properly processed.
    final void
    testThreePermute_1()
    Ensures that permuting the only three POs of a PO file is properly processed.
    final void

    Ensures that permuting the only three POs of a PO file is properly processed.
    final void

    Ensures that permuting the only three POs of a PO file is properly processed.
    final void

    Ensures that permuting the only three POs of a PO file is properly processed.
    final void
    testThreePermute_5()
    Ensures that permuting the only three POs of a PO file is properly processed.
    final void
    testTwo()
    Ensures that a PO file containing two POs is properly processed.
    final void
    testTwoAddAfter()
    Ensures that adding one PO after the two POs of a PO file is properly processed.
    final void
    testTwoAddBefore()
    Ensures that adding one PO before the two POs of a PO file is properly processed.
    final void
    testTwoAddBetween()
    Ensures that adding one PO between the two POs of a PO file is properly processed.
    final void
    testTwoAddThree()
    Ensures that adding three POs at every position in a PO file containing two POs is properly processed.
    final void
    testTwoDeleteAll()
    Ensures that deleting both POs of a PO file containing two POs is properly processed.
    final void
    testTwoDeleteFirst()
    Ensures that deleting the first PO of a PO file containing two POs is properly processed.

    testTwoDeleteLast()
    Ensures that deleting the last PO of a PO file containing two POs is properly processed.
    final void
    testTwoPermute()
    Ensures that permuting the only two POs of a PO file is properly processed.

      public final void testSixPermute_1() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Ensures that inverse permuting the only six POs of a PO file is properly processed.

      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException

    • testSixPermute_2

      public final void testSixPermute_2() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Ensures that permuting the only six POs of a PO file is properly processed (case with a random permutation with random deletions).
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException

    • testSixPermute_3

      public final void testSixPermute_3() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Ensures that permuting the only six POs of a PO file is properly processed (case with a random permutation with random deletions).
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException

    • testSixPermute_4

      public final void testSixPermute_4() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Ensures that permuting the only six POs of a PO file is properly processed (case with a random permutation with random deletions).
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException

    • testErroneousProofDependency

      public final void testErroneousProofDependency() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Ensures that a damaged proof dependency does not prevent POM from succeeding. The PO with the damaged proof is then marked as broken.
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException

    • testErroneousProof

      public final void testErroneousProof() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Ensures that a damaged proof does not prevent POM from succeeding. However, the PO with the damaged proof is not marked as broken if the proof dependencies match.
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException

    • testErroneousProofInput

      public final void testErroneousProofInput() throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Ensures that a proof with a damaged input does not prevent POM from succeeding. However, the PO with the damaged proof is not marked as broken if the proof dependencies match.
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException

    • specializedFactoryNotChanged

      public void specializedFactoryNotChanged() throws Exception
      Ensure that a proof is kept valid when updating its proof obligation without really changing it nor its factory, even when using a specialized factory.
      Throws:
      Exception

    • specializedFactoryChangedToDefault

      public void specializedFactoryChangedToDefault() throws Exception
      Ensure that a non-empty proof becomes invalid when updating a proof obligation without really changing it, if PO factory changes and that it gets recovered when resetting the PO factory.
      Throws:
      Exception

    • defaultFactoryChangedToSpecialized

      public void defaultFactoryChangedToSpecialized() throws Exception
      Ensure that a non-empty proof becomes invalid when updating a proof obligation without really changing it, if PO factory changes.
      Throws:
      Exception

    • specializedFactoryChangedToDefaultOnException

      public void specializedFactoryChangedToDefaultOnException() throws Exception
      Ensure that a non-empty proof becomes invalid when updating a proof obligation without really changing it, if PO factory can't be loaded and that it gets recovered when resetting the PO factory.
      Throws:
      Exception

    • emptyProofHasNofactory

      public void emptyProofHasNofactory() throws Exception
      Ensures that an empty proof has no formula factory. This is detected by changing the factory of the proof obligation and checking that the proof used instantaneously the same factory (without need to rebuild).
      Throws:
      Exception

    • testContextDependentProof

      public void testContextDependentProof() throws Exception
      Verify that context dependent proof statuses are updated when the builder runs, even if the PO stamp did not change.
      Throws:
      Exception
